perturbation.original_text: Base Servicing Fee: As to any Mortgage Loan and any Collection Period, an amount equal to the product of the Base Servicing Fee Rate, the UPB of that Mortgage Loan as of the related Measurement Date and 1/12 or, for the first Collection Period, the number of days in such Collection Period divided by 360; provided, however, that payment of the Base Servicing Fee for any delinquent Mortgage Loan shall be suspended unless and until Freedom Mortgage recovers the amount thereof from payments in respect thereof from the related mortgagor or the amount thereof is otherwise recovered from liquidation of the related property.
perturbation.changed_text: Base Servicing Fee: As to any Mortgage Loan and any Collection Period, an amount equal to the product of the Base Servicing Fee Rate, the UPB of that Mortgage Loan as of the related Measurement Date and 1/12 or, for the first Collection Period, the number of days in such Collection Period divided by 360; provided, however, that payment of the Base Servicing Fee for any delinquent Mortgage Loan shall always be paid regardless of whether Freedom Mortgage recovers the amount thereof from payments in respect thereof from the related mortgagor or the amount thereof is otherwise recovered from liquidation of the related property.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition suspends the Base Servicing Fee for delinquent mortgage loans until recovery. This change mandates payment regardless of recovery, creating an in-text contradiction. If other parts of the agreement rely on the servicing fee suspension to manage risk, this change directly conflicts with those risk management provisions within the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Base Servicing Fee: As to any Mortgage Loan and any Collection Period, an amount equal to the product of the Base Servicing Fee Rate, the UPB of that Mortgage Loan as of the related Measurement Date and 1/12 or, for the first Collection Period, the number of days in such Collection Period divided by 360; provided, however, that payment of the Base Servicing Fee for any delinquent Mortgage Loan shall always be paid regardless of whether Freedom Mortgage recovers the amount thereof from payments in respect thereof from the related mortgagor or the amount thereof is otherwise recovered from liquidation of the related property.
perturbation.justification: The original text suspends the Base Servicing Fee for delinquent mortgage loans until recovery from the mortgagor or liquidation of the property. The changed text mandates the Base Servicing Fee is always paid, regardless of recovery. This creates a direct contradiction because the two versions of the clause provide conflicting instructions regarding when the Base Servicing Fee is to be paid for delinquent loans. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Tangible Net Worth: The net worth of Seller determined in accordance with GAAP, minus all intangibles determined in accordance with GAAP (including goodwill, capitalized financing costs and capitalized administration costs but excluding originated and purchased mortgage servicing rights or retained residual securities) and any and all advances to, investments in and receivables held from affiliates; provided, however, that the non­cash effect (gain or loss) of any mark­to­market adjustments made directly to stockholders' equity for fluctuation of the value of financial instruments as mandated under the Statement of Financial Accounting Standards No. 133 (or any successor statement) shall be excluded from the calculation of Tangible Net Worth.
perturbation.changed_text: Tangible Net Worth: The net worth of Seller determined in accordance with GAAP, minus all intangibles determined in accordance with GAAP (including goodwill, capitalized financing costs and capitalized administration costs and including originated and purchased mortgage servicing rights or retained residual securities) and any and all advances to, investments in and receivables held from affiliates; provided, however, that the non­cash effect (gain or loss) of any mark­to­market adjustments made directly to stockholders' equity for fluctuation of the value of financial instruments as mandated under the Statement of Financial Accounting Standards No. 133 (or any successor statement) shall be included in the calculation of Tangible Net Worth.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of Tangible Net Worth excludes mortgage servicing rights and mark-to-market adjustments. This modification includes them, directly altering how Tangible Net Worth is calculated. This contradicts any clauses that rely on the original definition for financial thresholds or triggers, particularly Standby Trigger Event, leading to uncertainty about triggering events and creates an internal contradiction with the old values.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Tangible Net Worth: The net worth of Seller determined in accordance with GAAP, minus all intangibles determined in accordance with GAAP (including goodwill, capitalized financing costs and capitalized administration costs and including originated and purchased mortgage servicing rights or retained residual securities) and any and all advances to, investments in and receivables held from affiliates; provided, however, that the non­cash effect (gain or loss) of any mark­to­market adjustments made directly to stockholders' equity for fluctuation of the value of financial instruments as mandated under the Statement of Financial Accounting Standards No. 133 (or any successor statement) shall be included in the calculation of Tangible Net Worth.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the original definition of 'Tangible Net Worth' by including originated and purchased mortgage servicing rights and mark-to-market adjustments, where previously excluded. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the altered definition affects other sections, particularly those relying on the original definition for financial thresholds or triggering events.

perturbation.original_text: Section 5. Term and Termination. (a) Unless earlier terminated as provided below, this Agreement shall remain in effect until the later to occur of the date that is (x) three (3) years from the date hereof and (y) the date on which an affiliate of Freedom Mortgage is not acting as the external manager of Cherry Hill.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5. Term and Termination. (a) Unless earlier terminated as provided below, this Agreement shall remain in effect until the earlier to occur of the date that is (x) three (3) years from the date hereof and (y) the date on which an affiliate of Freedom Mortgage is not acting as the external manager of Cherry Hill.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5(a) originally states the agreement remains in effect until the *later* of two dates. The modification changes this to the *earlier* of the two dates. This fundamentally alters the term of the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction on how long the Term of the agreement will last, especially affecting downstream obligations related to the agreement, leading to a conflict between the two conditions
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5. Term and Termination. (a) Unless earlier terminated as provided below, this Agreement shall remain in effect until the earlier to occur of the date that is (x) three (3) years from the date hereof and (y) the date on which an affiliate of Freedom Mortgage is not acting as the external manager of Cherry Hill.
perturbation.justification: The modified text alters the fundamental term of the agreement from the *later* to the *earlier* of two dates, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text, which is identical to the Changed Text, now conflicts with the original intent of the agreement's duration, creating a direct in-text contradiction. This is because the original clause stipulated that the agreement would last until the *later* of the dates, thereby extending the term for as long as possible. The altered clause dramatically reverses this, stipulating that the agreement will last until the *earlier* of the dates, thereby shortening the term. As a result, downstream obligations would be impacted and be unenforceable if the term is determined by the *earlier* date of termination, versus the *later* date of termination.
